project_summary: Roebuck is a developer tooling project that performs AI-powered analysis of Git repositories, generating reports on pull requests, file history, code churn, releases, and repository profiles. It integrates with GitHub to retrieve repository data and uses the Claude AI API to produce narrative documentation and analytical reports, all driven through a Typer-based CLI.
architecture_notes: The project follows a layered architecture: a CLI layer (cli.py using Typer) dispatches commands to a set of pluggable analyser modules (churn, file_history, pr, profile, release), each exposing a uniform `run`-style function that accepts an `AppConfig` and returns a `Path` to the generated report. Configuration is centralised in a Pydantic BaseModel hierarchy loaded from a TOML file. A dedicated `ClaudeClient` class encapsulates all communication with the Claude AI API, keeping AI concerns isolated from the analyser logic. This design makes it straightforward to add new analysers without modifying the CLI or configuration layers.

public_modules.purpose: Analyses code churn across the repository and produces a churn report at a returned Path.
Analyses the Git history of a specific file and produces a file-history report at a returned Path.
Analyses a GitHub pull request by number and produces a PR analysis report at a returned Path.
Captures a structured repository profile and generates documentation from it, each returning a Path to the output artifact.
Analyses a release by comparing a tag against a base ref and produces a release report at a returned Path.
Provides the ClaudeClient class that wraps communication with the Anthropic Claude AI API.
Defines the Typer-based CLI entry point and command handlers that delegate to the analyser modules.
Defines the Pydantic configuration model hierarchy and the load_config function for reading config.toml.

public_interfaces.name: run
public_interfaces.signature: def run(path: str, cfg: AppConfig) -> Path

public_interfaces.name: run
public_interfaces.signature: def run(number: int, cfg: AppConfig) -> Path

public_interfaces.name: capture
public_interfaces.signature: def capture(cfg: AppConfig, force: bool=False) -> Path | None

public_interfaces.name: run
public_interfaces.signature: def run(tag: str, base: str | None, cfg: AppConfig) -> Path

public_interfaces.name: analyse_pr
public_interfaces.signature: def analyse_pr(number: int=typer.Argument(..., help='Pull request number'), config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT) -> None

public_interfaces.name: analyse_file
public_interfaces.signature: def analyse_file(path: str=typer.Argument(..., help='File path relative to repo root'), config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT) -> None

public_interfaces.name: analyse_release
public_interfaces.signature: def analyse_release(tag: str=typer.Argument(..., help='Release tag to analyse'), base: str=typer.Option(None, '--base', '-b', help='Base tag/ref to compare against (defaults to previous tag)'), config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT) -> None

public_interfaces.name: report_churn
public_interfaces.signature: def report_churn(config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT) -> None

public_interfaces.name: profile_capture
public_interfaces.signature: def profile_capture(config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT, force: bool=typer.Option(False, '--force', '-f', help='Overwrite existing profile.json')) -> None

public_interfaces.name: profile_generate_docs
public_interfaces.signature: def profile_generate_docs(config_path: Path=_CONFIG_OPT, repo: Optional[str]=_REPO_OPT) -> None

data_models.name: AppConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Top-level application configuration aggregating GitHubConfig, ClaudeConfig, SpecsConfig, ChurnConfig, ProfileConfig, ReportsConfig, and ContextConfig sub-models; loaded from a TOML file.

data_models.name: GitHubConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration for GitHub integration, likely containing repository owner/name and authentication token fields.

data_models.name: ClaudeConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration for the Claude AI client, likely containing model name, API key, and request parameters.

data_models.name: SpecsConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration related to specification or prompt templates used during analysis.

data_models.name: ChurnConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration controlling churn analysis parameters such as time window or file filters.

data_models.name: ProfileConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration for repository profile capture and documentation generation, likely including output paths.

data_models.name: ReportsConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration specifying output directories or formats for generated reports.

data_models.name: ContextConfig
data_models.fields_summary: Configuration providing contextual information about the repository or project supplied to AI prompts.

external_dependencies: Anthropic Claude AI API (via ClaudeClient, driven by ClaudeConfig)
GitHub API (repository, pull request, and release data retrieval, driven by GitHubConfig)
TOML configuration files (config.toml loaded by load_config)
